title: Stop-in-k-laps discrete hazard head with next-lap extraction
summary: Discrete-time survival literature recommends modeling event hazard as conditional probability over person-period data.
rationale: Discrete-time survival literature recommends modeling event hazard as conditional probability over person-period data. For pit strategy, predicting `stop in 1,2,3,...,H laps` is often easier than directly learning only `next lap`, and the `k=1` hazard becomes the submission target.
implementation_hint: Within each driver-race stint, derive future stop distance from observed `PitStop` rows; expand or relabel rows with horizon bins (for example `1..6`, `7+`, `no more stop`). Train a multiclass or multi-label hazard model using only past/current features, then use the `k=1` posterior as `PitNextLap`. Compare to a direct binary model under embargoed race-level CV.
expected_effect: Improves signal density near stops and should sharpen ordering around true pit windows.
risk: Label construction can silently leak if post-row stop information is merged incorrectly; horizon choice may overfit if too fine.
